conceptClusters.title: Retrieved semantic access paths
exitEvidence: Learner retrieves exact region, image, recovery, table-context, caption, transcript, and media-equivalence evidence before making broad accessibility claims.
Changed content preserves complete task paths instead of treating isolated attributes as accessibility proof.

conceptClusters.title: People tasks barriers strategies and outcomes
exitEvidence: Learner connects a specific changed task and observed barrier to possible access strategies, assistive technologies, adaptive settings, and relevant perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust outcomes without diagnosing or stereotyping a person.
Learner separates standards evidence, observed task evidence, assumptions, and unresolved risk.

conceptClusters.title: HTML to accessibility API exposure
exitEvidence: Learner predicts and inspects DOM element, content, attribute, and relationship effects on role, name, description, state, property, value, and structure.
Learner distinguishes browser accessibility exposure from any one assistive technology announcement and verifies changed native markup with styles removed.

conceptClusters.title: Visible labels accessible names and descriptions
exitEvidence: Learner chooses visible native labeling first, computes the controlling name source and precedence, keeps supplementary instructions and errors in descriptions, and refuses names on roles where they are harmful or prohibited.
Changed duplicate, icon-only, translated, referenced, hidden-reference, and descendant-content cases preserve concise distinguishable names and separate descriptions.

conceptClusters.title: Rendering accessibility-tree and focus inclusion
exitEvidence: Learner independently predicts rendering, layout, DOM presence, accessibility-tree exposure, focusability, sequential focus, form participation, naming reference, and task meaning for hidden, CSS-hidden, inert, aria-hidden, and visually-hidden content.
No focusable or uniquely meaningful task content remains inside an excluded subtree after changed states.

conceptClusters.title: Source reading visual and focus order
exitEvidence: Learner preserves a meaningful source and reading sequence, logical native sequential focus, visible unobscured focus, keyboard operation, escape, and continuation without positive tabindex or CSS-order repair.
Changed responsive layout and inserted controls preserve meaning and operation across linear, visual, reading, and focus traces.

conceptClusters.title: Native-first ARIA role state property and behavior contract
exitEvidence: Learner rejects unnecessary, conflicting, prohibited, unsupported, stale, or partial ARIA and names the native HTML alternative.

Any unavoidable custom interface proves complete role, name, state, property, value, relationship, keyboard, pointer, focus, disabled, activation, result, escape, touch, zoom, script-loss, and changed-state behavior.

conceptClusters.title: Complementary accessibility evaluation and limits
exitEvidence: Learner chooses standards inspection, automation, DOM and accessibility-tree inspection, keyboard, zoom, contrast, responsive, assistive-technology, and observed-user tasks according to the claim each can falsify.

Reports preserve scope, environment, task, expected and observed evidence, tool limits, user-reported barriers, repairs, retests, unresolved risk, and prohibit universal claims from one tool or participant.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked evidence map traces people and tasks through barriers, semantic exposure, names, inclusion, order, ARIA boundaries, and complementary evaluation.
activityDesigns.startingState: Changed task stories, native HTML, DOM and accessibility-tree snapshots, name computations, focus traces, ARIA diffs, and tool reports are provided without conclusions.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner predicts and explains all twelve new or retrieved concepts before guided repair.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback contrasts the committed prediction with one observable task, DOM, API, focus, behavior, or evaluation fact.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one causal link at a time and never supplies a universal disability-to-tool mapping.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires a revised claim and smaller changed case before continuing.

interactions.title: Retrieve region navigation
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect banner, navigation, main, complementary, and contentinfo exposure and naming in a changed multi-region page without inventing redundant landmarks.

evidence.observable: Purposeful regions expose correct native roles, unique names only when needed, and one main task path.
evidence.changedCase: A second navigation region is inserted with overlapping links.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every container needs a landmark role and name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map task regions before roles and names.

interactions.title: Retrieve image purpose
interactions.learnerAction: Predict informative, functional, decorative, complex, text-image, and redundant image alternatives after surrounding purpose and linked action change.

evidence.observable: Alternative exposes equivalent task meaning or empty decoration without duplicating adjacent content.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative seal becomes the only linked election-status control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Image appearance determines alternative text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify contextual purpose and task first.

interactions.title: Retrieve error recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect persistent specific errors, programmatic control relationships, retained valid values, summary navigation, correction, and single-request success in a changed form journey.

evidence.observable: Every error is perceivable, locatable, correctable, and cleared without duplicate submission.
evidence.changedCase: Two simultaneous invalid fields become valid in reverse order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Red border and alert announcement prove recovery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace full error-to-correction task.

interactions.title: Retrieve table context
interactions.learnerAction: Predict exact row, column, group, and explicit header context for every changed data cell instead of inferring relationships from visual position.

evidence.observable: Each data cell receives all intended actual headers and no unrelated header.
evidence.changedCase: A grouped election-results column becomes an irregular subcolumn.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Bold first row supplies all table context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive every cell association from structure.

interactions.title: Retrieve media equivalence
interactions.learnerAction: Predict captions, transcripts, essential visual-information treatment, controls, and fallback needs for changed prerecorded, audio-only, video-only, and synchronized media tasks.

evidence.observable: Task-specific speech, non-speech audio, and essential visuals have synchronized or persistent equivalents as required.
evidence.changedCase: A silent map animation gains spoken route changes and alerts.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any transcript replaces all timed alternatives.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate media mode, information, timing, and task.

interactions.title: Map task barriers without stereotypes
interactions.learnerAction: Connect changed visual, auditory, physical, speech, cognitive, language, neurological, temporary, and situational task conditions to possible barriers, strategies, tools, and POUR outcomes without assigning one tool to one person.
interactions.artifactChange: A bounded task-barrier-strategy map is committed.

evidence.observable: Claims distinguish person, task, context, barrier, possible strategy, standards outcome, observation, assumption, and uncertainty.
evidence.changedCase: Bright quiet desktop voting research changes to noisy mobile zoom use with one hand.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Disability label determines required technology.
interactions.correctionRoute: Start with observed task and barrier, not diagnosis.

interactions.title: Trace native accessibility exposure
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect how changed HTML element, content, attributes, value, state, and relationships map through the browser to role, name, description, state, property, value, and structure.
interactions.artifactChange: A DOM-to-API exposure trace is saved.

evidence.observable: Trace separates source, parsed DOM, computed semantic object, platform exposure, and observed presentation.
evidence.changedCase: A native button becomes a styled generic element with button text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible appearance equals accessibility information.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each layer and mark the first divergence.

interactions.title: Compute names and descriptions
interactions.learnerAction: Predict accessible name and separate description from visible text, native labels, descendant content, alt, caption, aria-labelledby, aria-label, aria-describedby, title, and placeholder under changed precedence.
interactions.artifactChange: Name and description computations are committed.

evidence.observable: Exact concise distinguishing name and supplementary description identify their controlling sources and ignored sources.
evidence.changedCase: Translated visible label changes while stale aria-label and help text remain.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All available text concatenates into a better name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply native-first source and precedence rules.

interactions.title: Predict independent inclusion dimensions
interactions.learnerAction: Predict rendering, layout, DOM presence, accessibility-tree exposure, focusability, sequential focus, form participation, naming reference, and task meaning for hidden, display-none, visibility-hidden, inert, aria-hidden, and visually hidden variants.

evidence.observable: Each mechanism has correct independent effects and no excluded subtree retains a focusable or unique task path.
evidence.changedCase: A collapsed error summary still contains a focusable correction link.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All hiding mechanisms remove the same things.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate each rendering, tree, focus, form, and meaning dimension.

interactions.title: Trace meaningful and focus order
interactions.learnerAction: Predict source, linear reading, visual, accessibility, sequential focus, and interaction order after responsive reflow, CSS reordering, control insertion, hidden states, and tabindex changes.

evidence.observable: Meaning and operation remain coherent with native focus, visible unobscured indicators, no trap, and no positive tabindex repair.
evidence.changedCase: A two-column ballot guide stacks and gains an urgent action control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Matching visual order or numeric tabindex proves logical navigation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Make source order meaningful, then verify every presentation.

interactions.title: Bound ARIA to complete behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Choose native HTML, no semantic override, justified ARIA relationship, or a fully implemented custom interface after inspecting role, name, state, property, value, keyboard, focus, pointer, disabled, result, escape, touch, zoom, and script-loss behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: ARIA acceptance and refusal decisions are committed.

evidence.observable: ARIA is necessary, conforming, supported, synchronized, and paired with every promised behavior or is removed for native HTML.
evidence.changedCase: A generic election toggle gains role and aria-pressed but no keyboard handler.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Correct role and state automatically create widget behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prefer native behavior or enumerate and prove the full promise.

interactions.title: Choose evidence that can falsify claims
interactions.learnerAction: Choose standards inspection, automation, source and DOM review, accessibility-tree inspection, keyboard, zoom, responsive, contrast, assistive-technology, and observed-user tasks for changed claims, labeling what each cannot establish.

evidence.observable: Every claim has a reproducible capable check, stated limit, expected result, changed case, repair path, and unresolved risk.
evidence.changedCase: Zero automated findings accompany a failed keyboard application task.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One score, tool, browser, assistive technology, or participant proves access.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask what evidence could disprove this exact claim.

interactions.title: Explain one causal accessibility chain
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how changed task context flows through source, DOM, semantic exposure, name, inclusion, order, input behavior, output, and observed task outcome without collapsing layers.
interactions.artifactChange: A complete causal chain becomes a model checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Every claim names mechanism, observable consequence, changed evidence, and remaining uncertainty.
evidence.changedCase: A visual-only urgent status becomes an interactive dismissible notice.

interactions.feedbackTarget: WCAG label or screen-reader narration substitutes for causal evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild from task to first divergent technical layer.

interactions.title: Defend complementary evidence limits
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why standard conformance, automated findings, simulations, one assistive technology, and one observed participant each contribute evidence yet cannot alone prove universal usability, then propose bounded next evidence.

evidence.observable: Conclusion matches sample, tools, environments, tasks, expertise, observed barriers, and untested risk without overclaim.
evidence.changedCase: A second participant and browser contradict the first successful task result.

interactions.feedbackTarget: More tools automatically convert findings into conformance proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound every conclusion to what was actually evaluated.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An election-information service is audited and repaired with reproducible task evidence across semantic, keyboard, zoom, name, inclusion, media, form, table, and evaluation paths.
activityDesigns.startingState: A nonpartisan fictional election-information page contains evidence-rich but independently authored barriers across every cluster.
activityDesigns.completionState: All guided repairs pass changed task, source, DOM, accessibility-tree, keyboard, responsive, media, form, table, and complementary-evaluation checks.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names observed evidence, unmet competency, consequence, and smallest next action without giving finished code.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints progress from task observation to structure, exposure, behavior, and only then a bounded repair direction.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failed evidence routes to a smaller parallel election-information case and a fresh changed recheck.

interactions.title: Predict landmark task paths
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how changed region structure and repeated navigation affect finding registration, polling, ballot, and result information through region navigation, headings, search, and linear reading.

evidence.observable: Each task has a purposeful region path without redundant or unnamed repeated landmarks.
evidence.changedCase: A local-results navigation and emergency complementary region are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: More landmarks always reduce navigation effort.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map task destinations and repeated-region distinction.

interactions.title: Predict image-dependent barriers
interactions.learnerAction: Predict task loss for changed ballot icons, district maps, status badges, candidate photos, decorative seals, and image links across nonvisual, low-vision, text-only, slow-resource, and cognitive-support use.

evidence.observable: Each contextual purpose maps to equivalent meaning or empty decoration and a falsifiable task check.
evidence.changedCase: A district color key becomes the only source of precinct eligibility.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One detailed description fits every image user and purpose.
interactions.correctionRoute: Start from contextual task information and action.

interactions.title: Predict recovery barriers
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how simultaneous registration lookup errors affect keyboard, zoom, speech-input, screen-reader, memory, language, and interrupted-session tasks without treating a tool as a person.

evidence.observable: Specific barrier, strategy, technical evidence, assumption, and uncertainty remain separate for each changed task.
evidence.changedCase: Three errors appear after a network retry and one valid value is cleared.

interactions.feedbackTarget: An assertive alert fixes every error-recovery barrier.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace perceive, locate, understand, correct, and resubmit actions.

interactions.title: Predict table-navigation barriers
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how changed precinct-result groups, spans, and headers affect scan, zoom, copy, linear reading, table navigation, and memory-heavy comparison tasks.

evidence.observable: Barriers are tied to exact cell context, navigation action, content density, and changed relationship.
evidence.changedCase: Mail and election-day totals split into grouped subcolumns.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Horizontal scrolling is the only table accessibility concern.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test exact comparison tasks and cell contexts.

interactions.title: Predict media-equivalence barriers
interactions.learnerAction: Predict barriers and evidence for a changed candidate-forum video across absent audio, absent visuals, noisy context, quiet context, language change, reading difficulty, timing, and keyboard control.

evidence.observable: Speech, non-speech sound, visual information, timing, control, language, and task each receive bounded evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A silent chart animation gains spoken numbers and unscripted audience questions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Captions or transcript alone prove complete media access.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate information channels, timing, controls, and task.

interactions.title: Inspect diverse election tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect exact find, compare, understand, complete, correct, and confirm tasks under changed input, output, zoom, motion, time, language, literacy, distraction, fatigue, and connection contexts.

evidence.observable: Records describe observable task barriers and possible strategies without diagnosis, stereotype, or universal claim.
evidence.changedCase: Desktop pointer use changes to keyboard-only use at four-hundred-percent zoom.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A persona label is sufficient evidence of user need.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record action, context, barrier, strategy, evidence, and uncertainty.

interactions.title: Repair one multi-context task path
interactions.learnerAction: Code the smallest semantic and content repair that preserves one changed election-information task across keyboard, zoom, text-only, speech-input naming, and interrupted use without special user modes.
interactions.artifactChange: A multi-context task repair is committed.

evidence.observable: One ordinary interface exposes complete perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust task evidence across contexts.
evidence.changedCase: The task gains a second region, longer translated labels, and a missing image.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Separate accessibility mode is safer than robust shared content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the common causal barrier in the primary interface.

interactions.title: Explain bounded people evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Explain which election-task conclusions come from standards, inspection, simulation, observed behavior, assumptions, or unknowns and why one disability, tool, or success cannot generalize.

evidence.observable: Every conclusion is scoped to actual task, context, participant or tool, and evidence source with explicit remaining risk.
evidence.changedCase: A successful expert screen-reader task conflicts with a novice keyboard user's failure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any disabled participant validates the whole interface.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound the conclusion and request complementary evidence.

interactions.title: Predict election semantic objects
interactions.learnerAction: Predict roles, names, descriptions, states, properties, values, levels, set relationships, and regions for changed native headings, links, buttons, forms, tables, details, media, and status content.

evidence.observable: Predicted object information follows actual HTML mapping and content relationships rather than CSS appearance.
evidence.changedCase: A heading becomes styled paragraph and native disclosure becomes generic containers.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tag name or visual style directly equals all announced output.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace element and attributes through browser mapping.

interactions.title: Inspect source DOM and tree separately
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect authored source, parser-repaired DOM, computed style, accessibility tree, platform properties, and observed assistive-technology output for the changed election page without treating any layer as identical.

evidence.observable: First divergence and browser mapping are identified before tool-specific presentation differences.
evidence.changedCase: Invalid nested controls are parser-repaired and one role is suppressed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: DOM inspector or screen-reader speech alone is the accessibility tree.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record each layer, environment, and mapping separately.

interactions.title: Restore native election structure
interactions.learnerAction: Replace style-only headings, regions, links, buttons, groups, table headers, and disclosure containers with the simplest truthful native HTML while preserving content and task behavior.

evidence.observable: Expected roles, relationships, states, names, values, and keyboard behavior emerge without redundant ARIA.
evidence.changedCase: Repeated regions and one grouped results table are inserted after repair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding roles to generic elements is equivalent to native HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose the native element from task behavior and relationship.

interactions.title: Test semantic state changes
interactions.learnerAction: Activate details, form controls, validation, media, and status changes; capture role, name, state, value, relationship, focus, and task result before and after each transition.

evidence.observable: Exposed states and values synchronize with rendered, DOM, focus, submission, playback, and task outcomes.
evidence.changedCase: A validation retry, disclosure close, and media-source switch occur together.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Initial semantic snapshot proves dynamic behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare every precondition, action, transition, exposure, and result.

interactions.title: Test style removal and zoom
interactions.learnerAction: Remove author styles, zoom, enlarge text, narrow the viewport, change contrast preferences, and verify semantic structure, content, focus, control identity, order, and complete task remain available.

evidence.observable: Native information and operation survive presentation changes without CSS-generated essential content.
evidence.changedCase: Text triples, columns stack, images fail, and generated labels disappear.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Correct accessibility tree makes rendered reflow irrelevant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test semantic exposure and perceivable presentation together.

interactions.title: Inspect relationship completeness
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect heading levels, landmark ownership, label-control pairs, descriptions, error references, fieldset groups, table header contexts, media alternatives, and disclosure ownership as a changed relationship graph.

evidence.observable: Every reference resolves uniquely, matches visible meaning, remains in scope, and exposes intended structure.
evidence.changedCase: IDs are renamed, one control moves forms, and one header group splits.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Valid tokens prove correct relationships.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve each source, target, scope, meaning, and changed consumer.

interactions.title: Repair exposed status and progress
interactions.learnerAction: Code persistent visible result text and a bounded native or ARIA status mechanism only where necessary, without stealing focus, duplicating announcements, or hiding prior context.

evidence.observable: Changed status is visible, exposed once at the right urgency, reviewable later, and leaves focus and task context stable.
evidence.changedCase: A slow lookup emits progress, partial results, retry, and final success.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Live region presence guarantees useful notification.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate visible persistence, exposure, urgency, timing, and focus.

interactions.title: Explain browser mapping limits
interactions.learnerAction: Explain which election semantics are author-controlled, browser-computed, platform-exposed, assistive-technology-presented, environment-dependent, or still unverified after changed native markup.

evidence.observable: Claims identify exact responsible layer, current environment, observable evidence, and unsupported inference.
evidence.changedCase: Two browser and assistive-technology combinations expose one state differently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Specification mapping guarantees identical spoken output everywhere.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound normative mapping separately from observed presentation.

interactions.title: Predict visible native labels
interactions.learnerAction: Predict accessible names and activation areas for election search, district input, ballot choice groups, result tables, figures, regions, links, and buttons using visible native text first.

evidence.observable: Each role has an appropriate concise distinguishing name from the intended visible native source.
evidence.changedCase: Duplicate Search controls and two result navigation regions are added.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Nearby text visually labels every object.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use role-appropriate native association and visible source.

interactions.title: Inspect name precedence conflicts
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect computed names when descendant text, label, legend, alt, caption, aria-labelledby, aria-label, title, placeholder, and hidden referenced content conflict or change language.

evidence.observable: Winning source, concatenation order, ignored content, role allowance, visible-label match, and changed result are exact.
evidence.changedCase: A translated button retains stale aria-label and references hidden district text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Browser combines all useful sources automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply exact precedence and role naming rules.

interactions.title: Repair duplicate action names
interactions.learnerAction: Code concise visible-first names that distinguish repeated edit, download, compare, register, and result links by purpose and context without adding role words or hidden verbosity.
interactions.artifactChange: Repeated election actions gain distinct maintainable names.

evidence.observable: Links-only and controls-only lists distinguish every action while visible and computed wording stay aligned.
evidence.changedCase: A second district with matching action labels is inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Long surrounding paragraph should become every action name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Lead with action and add only distinguishing context.

interactions.title: Separate instructions and errors
interactions.learnerAction: Keep control purpose in the visible native label; associate concise format help, eligibility conditions, current errors, and recovery guidance as separate descriptions or group context without name inflation.
interactions.artifactChange: Lookup names and descriptions are repaired.

evidence.observable: Name identifies control; descriptions expose current supplementary information in stable meaningful order.
evidence.changedCase: Help text changes language and one field gains a specific server error.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All instructions and errors belong inside aria-label.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate identity from supplementary and changing information.

interactions.title: Test icon and image names
interactions.learnerAction: Test icon-only controls, image links, candidate photos, decorative marks, district maps, and status icons with computed-name, links-only, images-off, language, and changed-purpose checks.
interactions.artifactChange: Image and icon naming evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Functional purpose remains concise and distinguishable; informative meaning remains equivalent; decoration remains absent.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative star becomes the only favorite-candidate toggle control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Icon filename or visible tooltip proves a good name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reclassify contextual purpose then inspect computed result.

interactions.title: Test label-in-name and translation
interactions.learnerAction: Compare persistent visible labels with computed names under speech input, reordered words, punctuation, abbreviation, localization, text enlargement, and stale invisible strings.
interactions.artifactChange: Label-in-name and translation evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Visible label text appears in the accessible name in expected reading order and all invisible strings share language and maintenance path.
evidence.changedCase: English Register to vote becomes a longer translated action phrase.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Equivalent synonym always supports voice activation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Align computed identity with visible task wording.

interactions.title: Inspect descendant-content replacement
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect links, buttons, headings, table cells, options, and custom widgets where aria-label or aria-labelledby replaces descendant semantic content, then identify lost names, roles, structure, or visible meaning.

evidence.observable: Override is removed unless hiding descendant content is intentional, role-allowed, complete, and tested.
evidence.changedCase: A results link gains a hidden label that suppresses date and district text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ARIA naming only adds metadata and cannot hide content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect role naming-from-content and computed descendants.

interactions.title: Explain every election name
interactions.learnerAction: Explain source, precedence, role allowance, brevity, distinction, visible alignment, description boundary, language, changed behavior, and test evidence for every named election object.
interactions.artifactChange: Election naming decision record is committed.

evidence.observable: Each naming choice ties exact task identity to computed evidence and rejects lower-quality or harmful alternatives.
evidence.changedCase: Labels, repeated controls, errors, captions, and region count all change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute inventory substitutes for computed-name evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute one object from role and visible purpose.

interactions.title: Predict hidden-state dimensions
interactions.learnerAction: Predict rendering, layout, DOM, accessibility tree, direct and sequential focus, form participation, naming references, find-in-page, and task meaning for every hidden mechanism in the election service.

evidence.observable: Every mechanism has exact independent dimension outcomes rather than one generic hidden state.
evidence.changedCase: A collapsed eligibility panel gains a required field and focusable help link.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invisible and accessibility-hidden always mean the same thing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate one dimension at a time from actual mechanism.

interactions.title: Inspect aria-hidden descendants
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect every aria-hidden subtree for focusable descendants, active elements, referenced labels or descriptions, live updates, unique text, errors, status, and task actions before accepting exclusion.

evidence.observable: Excluded subtrees contain only intentional redundancy or decoration and no focus or unique task information.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative wrapper receives a retry button and current precinct status.

interactions.feedbackTarget: aria-hidden on parent affects only the parent's role.
interactions.correctionRoute: Audit every descendant and external reference.

interactions.title: Repair collapsible panel inclusion
interactions.learnerAction: Code disclosure, hidden, or inert state so a closed voter-help panel leaves no interactive descendants in sequential focus and an open panel restores complete visible, semantic, and keyboard access.

evidence.observable: Rendering, tree, focus, control state, relationships, and task access synchronize in both states.
evidence.changedCase: Panel closes while focus is inside and then reopens after content changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Offscreen positioning or aria-hidden alone closes an interactive panel.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use native state and manage focus only when task requires it.

interactions.title: Repair duplicate visual content
interactions.learnerAction: Hide only a redundant decorative icon or duplicate label from accessibility exposure while preserving visible content, functional name, focus indicator, status, and all unique meaning.

evidence.observable: Exactly one complete semantic representation remains and no task information or focus path disappears.
evidence.changedCase: The icon becomes functional while adjacent text changes to supplementary status.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated visible text is always safe to hide.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reclassify purpose after every content or behavior change.

interactions.title: Test focus against inclusion
interactions.learnerAction: Traverse forward, backward, direct-link, scripted, error-summary, and restored focus while opening, closing, hiding, disabling, and removing election content; compare rendered and accessibility inclusion.

evidence.observable: Focus never enters excluded content, disappears without recovery, or lands on an unavailable or unnamed task object.
evidence.changedCase: A hidden error target is removed while focus restoration and retry occur.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tab order alone covers all focus entry paths.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test each entry, transition, destination, and return path.

interactions.title: Test referenced hidden text
interactions.learnerAction: Change hidden and visible text referenced by aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, error relationships, captions, and controls; inspect computed strings, visibility, language, staleness, and task usefulness.

evidence.observable: Intentional references compute exact current text without concealing required visible information or creating maintenance divergence.
evidence.changedCase: Referenced eligibility help is translated, hidden, duplicated, and then removed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tree exclusion prevents content from contributing to any name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test reference computation separately from direct exposure.

interactions.title: Inspect visually hidden techniques
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect clipping, offscreen, transparent, zero-size, overflow, transform, and visually-hidden utilities for rendering, zoom, forced colors, focus, pointer, speech, copy, print, and unique-content consequences.

evidence.observable: Technique is accepted only for a bounded purpose and remains perceivable when focus or task meaning requires it.
evidence.changedCase: A hidden skip link receives focus at high zoom and forced colors.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any common utility class is universally safe.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify actual purpose and every affected modality.

interactions.title: Explain election inclusion decisions
interactions.learnerAction: Explain every hidden, inert, excluded, decorative, collapsed, conditionally rendered, and visually hidden election node through rendering, tree, focus, form, naming, and changed-task evidence.

evidence.observable: Each mechanism has one explicit purpose, complete dimensional evidence, mutation result, and refusal of unsafe alternatives.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative group becomes interactive and a form step becomes conditionally unavailable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hidden attribute names substitute for behavior evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild one decision across all independent dimensions.

interactions.title: Predict five election orders
interactions.learnerAction: Predict source, meaningful reading, visual, accessibility-tree, and sequential focus orders for changed header, navigation, main, results, aside, form, disclosure, and footer content.

evidence.observable: Every meaningful dependency and operation remains coherent without assuming all orders must be identical.
evidence.changedCase: Desktop sidebars stack between form fields and their instructions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual top-left sequence defines all correct orders.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify dependencies, task progression, and each presentation separately.

interactions.title: Inspect native sequential focus
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect focusable elements, native tab order, tabindex values, hidden and disabled states, nested controls, duplicate focus stops, focus indicators, viewport position, and surrounding context.

evidence.observable: Only meaningful operable objects receive focus in logical order with visible unobscured indicators.
evidence.changedCase: Positive tabindex, nested button link, and hidden focusable control are introduced.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every semantic object should be tabbable.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use native focusability from actual interaction behavior.

interactions.title: Repair source before presentation
interactions.learnerAction: Reorder election source so instructions precede fields, headings introduce sections, disclosures own content, results follow queries, and status follows action before applying responsive layout.

evidence.observable: Linearized and styled presentations preserve dependencies, task context, and native focus without duplicate content.
evidence.changedCase: Results move visually beside the query and a help panel enters the layout.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS order safely repairs semantically scrambled source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Make the unstyled source tell the task correctly first.

interactions.title: Remove tabindex repair
interactions.learnerAction: Remove positive tabindex and unnecessary zero tabindex, restore native elements and logical source order, and keep programmatic focus moves limited to task-required transitions with stable return targets.
interactions.artifactChange: Native focus order and bounded focus management are committed.

evidence.observable: Forward and reverse focus remain logical after insertions without numeric maintenance or static-text clutter.
evidence.changedCase: A new urgent registration action and conditional error summary are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Unique tabindex numbers create durable keyboard order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair source and native semantics, then justify each focus move.

interactions.title: Test complete keyboard tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Complete find, compare, expand, play, enter, correct, submit, confirm, reverse, cancel, escape, and resume tasks using keyboard only while recording key, focus, state, result, and continuation.

evidence.observable: Every function is operable without trap, unexpected context change, inaccessible hover path, or lost continuation.
evidence.changedCase: A timed status refresh and conditional form section occur during navigation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tabbing through controls proves keyboard operation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Exercise every action, state transition, escape, and return path.

interactions.title: Test focus visibility and obstruction
interactions.learnerAction: Track focus at narrow width, two-hundred and four-hundred-percent zoom, enlarged text, sticky headers, local table scroll, disclosures, errors, and status overlays using keyboard and touch-keyboard paths.
interactions.artifactChange: Focus visibility and obstruction evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Focused component remains visible, identifiable, unobscured by author content, and contextually understandable.
evidence.changedCase: A sticky election alert grows to three lines while the viewport narrows.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Browser focus ring guarantees perceivable focus.
interactions.correctionRoute: Observe indicator, clipping, contrast, scroll, and obstruction at each state.

interactions.title: Inspect responsive order mutation
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect grid, flex, absolute, fixed, sticky, display-contents, and generated-content effects on visual, reading, focus, hit-testing, and accessibility order without inferring support from appearance.
interactions.artifactChange: Responsive order mutation findings are saved.

evidence.observable: Changed layout preserves meaningful source and logical operation across supported browser and assistive-technology evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Cards reverse visually, one item becomes fixed, and controls wrap to new rows.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS layout never affects accessibility or interaction order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each actual browser presentation and source dependency.

interactions.title: Explain election order system
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why source, reading, visual, accessibility, and focus orders align or legitimately differ for every election task, including insertion, hiding, responsive reflow, error, and return-focus cases.

evidence.observable: Every dependency and divergence has task evidence, changed-case proof, and no positive-tabindex or duplicate-content patch.
evidence.changedCase: Form, results, navigation, and help regions reorder across two breakpoints.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One ideal universal order can be asserted without tasks.
interactions.correctionRoute: Defend each dependency and operation in each presentation.

interactions.title: Predict native versus ARIA choices
interactions.learnerAction: Choose native HTML, native HTML with supported ARIA, justified custom pattern, or rejection for election links, buttons, checkboxes, disclosures, tabs, menus, grids, status, alerts, and dialogs.

evidence.observable: Each accepted ARIA use fills a real semantic gap without overriding truthful native behavior or promising missing interaction.
evidence.changedCase: A visual button becomes toggle-like and a link is restyled as a command.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ARIA is more accessible than ordinary HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Start with exact task behavior and native equivalent.

interactions.title: Inspect role and property conformance
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect implicit and explicit roles, prohibited overrides, required and supported states, global properties, ownership, relationships, duplicate IDs, token validity, current state, and browser exposure.

evidence.observable: Every role and attribute is allowed, necessary, resolving, current, and semantically consistent with task and native host language.
evidence.changedCase: Role changes while stale aria-checked and aria-controls remain.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Validator-clean ARIA proves useful behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify role contract, host conflict, value, target, state, and behavior.

interactions.title: Replace partial custom controls
interactions.learnerAction: Replace generic election actions, checkboxes, disclosure buttons, selects, and links with native elements that supply activation, keyboard, focus, disabled, value, form, and state behavior automatically.
interactions.artifactChange: Partial custom controls become native controls.

evidence.observable: Native role, name, state, property, keyboard, pointer, touch, focus, form, and changed behavior all pass.
evidence.changedCase: A disabled state, form reset, and keyboard activation are introduced.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding keydown to role button completes all native behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use native element matching actual action and state.

interactions.title: Implement one justified relationship
interactions.learnerAction: Add only a necessary state, description, error, current-item, expanded, controlled, or live-status relationship to native election HTML and keep it synchronized with visible state and behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: One bounded ARIA relationship is committed.

evidence.observable: Attribute is role-supported, exact, resolving, current, visible-equivalent, behavior-synchronized, and removed when no longer true.
evidence.changedCase: The controlled panel is replaced and the error clears after retry.

interactions.feedbackTarget: More ARIA relationships create more context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Add only the semantic relation native HTML cannot express.

interactions.title: Test full custom promise
interactions.learnerAction: For one unavoidable custom election pattern, test role, name, description, state, property, value, ownership, reading, focus entry, internal keys, activation, pointer, touch, disabled, escape, result, return, zoom, and script loss.

evidence.observable: Every promised semantic and interaction behavior passes changed states or the pattern is rejected for a native simpler design.
evidence.changedCase: Items reorder, one disables, labels translate, and scripting fails.

interactions.feedbackTarget: APG role and keyboard list are a copy-ready guarantee.
interactions.correctionRoute: Treat the role as a promise and test every behavior in context.

interactions.title: Test ARIA state synchronization
interactions.learnerAction: Change expanded, pressed, checked, selected, current, invalid, busy, disabled, hidden, and live states through every keyboard, pointer, reset, error, loading, cancel, and retry path; compare visual, DOM, API, and task state.

evidence.observable: All state representations transition atomically, expose correct values, and return to valid defaults without stale claims.
evidence.changedCase: A request fails, retries, cancels, resets, and succeeds from another control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Initial attribute values prove state management.
interactions.correctionRoute: Build a transition table for every input and exit path.

interactions.title: Inspect enhancement and script loss
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect election content before enhancement, during loading, after success, after partial script failure, and with script unavailable; verify native content, navigation, forms, media, and status remain honest and usable where promised.

evidence.observable: Semantics never claim unavailable behavior and essential task paths have truthful bounded operation or explicit unavailability.
evidence.changedCase: A custom results filter initializes halfway and then loses its event handler.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ARIA semantics are harmless when behavior fails.
interactions.correctionRoute: Align exposed promise to actual behavior at every lifecycle state.

interactions.title: Defend every ARIA use and refusal
interactions.learnerAction: Explain semantic gap, native alternatives, role allowance, name, state, property, relationships, interaction, focus, support, change synchronization, failure behavior, tests, and residual risk for each election ARIA decision.

evidence.observable: Every retained attribute is necessary and proven; every partial, redundant, conflicting, or unsupported use is removed.
evidence.changedCase: Product requirements add a state, remove a behavior, and change browser support.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Conforming syntax or APG resemblance completes the defense.
interactions.correctionRoute: Re-evaluate task, native option, complete promise, and actual support.

interactions.title: Match claims to capable evidence
interactions.learnerAction: For election claims about semantics, names, inclusion, keyboard, zoom, contrast, media, forms, tables, usability, compatibility, and conformance, predict which evidence can falsify each and what remains untested.

evidence.observable: Each claim has suitable reproducible complementary evidence and explicit limits rather than one aggregate score.
evidence.changedCase: Automation passes while media, keyboard, and table tasks fail.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Run every tool and combine scores into certainty.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask what observation would disprove each exact claim.

interactions.title: Inspect automated findings critically
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect automated passes, failures, needs-review items, rules, applicability, DOM target, environment, false-positive hypothesis, false-negative boundary, severity, and required human decision.

evidence.observable: Findings are reproduced and interpreted without equating zero findings, score, or rule coverage with conformance or task success.
evidence.changedCase: Tool version changes and one rule stops applying to a modified component.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tool label and impact rating are final truth.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reproduce target, rule preconditions, observation, and human decision.

interactions.title: Run structured manual checks
interactions.learnerAction: Run documented source, semantics, name, inclusion, reading order, keyboard, focus, zoom, reflow, contrast, media, form, table, language, and changed-state checks with expected and observed results.

evidence.observable: Setup, scope, steps, expected, observed, pass/fail, evidence artifact, repair, retest, limit, and risk are reproducible.
evidence.changedCase: Content, viewport, input method, language, and one browser version change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Manual checkmark without evidence is sufficient.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record reproducible task and observable outcome.

interactions.title: Run bounded assistive-technology tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Run named find, navigate, compare, enter, correct, play, and confirm tasks in documented browser, platform, and assistive-technology combinations; record commands, output, result, barriers, and environment limits.

evidence.observable: Results are task- and environment-specific, distinguish author and interoperability hypotheses, and never generalize one combination.
evidence.changedCase: A second browser and input mode produce a different navigation result.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One screen-reader narration proves all assistive technology support.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound environment, task, observation, cause hypothesis, and next check.

interactions.title: Plan respectful observed-user evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Plan consented task-based evaluation with relevant disabled participants, accessible study materials, accommodations, privacy, compensation, facilitator neutrality, stopping conditions, qualitative reporting, and no diagnostic or universal inference.

evidence.observable: Plan complements standards review, matches target tasks and experience, protects participants, and bounds conclusions to sample and context.
evidence.changedCase: Target audience changes from expert staff to novice public applicants.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any disabled tester represents all users and conformance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match participants, tasks, ethics, scope, and complementary standards evidence.

interactions.title: Repair from combined evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Choose the smallest election artifact repair supported by standards, technical inspection, task behavior, and bounded user evidence; preserve correct paths and add a changed regression check.
interactions.artifactChange: Evidence-driven repair and regression are committed.

evidence.observable: Repair addresses causal barrier, passes original and changed tasks, avoids overfitting one tool, and records unresolved risk.
evidence.changedCase: The original participant succeeds but a changed keyboard and zoom task regresses.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Highest-severity tool finding must be repaired first regardless of task.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prioritize causal impact, risk, reach, and evidence confidence.

interactions.title: Retest complete election flow
interactions.learnerAction: Repeat original and adversarial find, compare, media, form, table, keyboard, zoom, inclusion, ARIA, persistence, and confirmation tasks after repairs; compare evidence and detect collateral regressions.

evidence.observable: Every prior barrier has repair and retest evidence; unchanged paths remain correct; failures and unknowns remain visible.
evidence.changedCase: Election content doubles, media language changes, and one form step becomes optional.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Fix verification can skip unrelated prior behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Retest affected dependency graph and representative complete tasks.

interactions.title: Defend bounded election conclusion
interactions.learnerAction: Defend what the election service evidence supports about specific standards, behaviors, environments, tasks, and observed users; state what it does not prove, remaining risks, owners, and next evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Bounded election accessibility report is committed.

evidence.observable: Conclusion is reproducible, traceable, non-universal, free of vanity scores, and proportionate to evidence quality and coverage.
evidence.changedCase: A new browser, policy requirement, and user-reported barrier arrive after signoff.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Passing checklist supports accessible certification language.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reduce claim to verified scope and schedule missing evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A library-service flow transfers the same evidence models with fewer prompts and unrelated content, structure, interactions, and defects.
activityDesigns.startingState: A partially complete library catalog, room-booking flow, event media, and service-status page include only goals and evidence fixtures.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner completes all clusters and explains remaining uncertainty with faded support.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports the first divergent observable and competency, not the repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: One optional cue names only the evidence layer to inspect.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss returns one smaller parallel library state before retry.

interactions.title: Complete library region-task map
interactions.learnerAction: Complete a changed find-book, reserve-room, renew-loan, and contact-library map connecting region navigation, barriers, possible strategies, evidence, assumptions, and POUR outcomes.

evidence.observable: Task-specific barriers and strategies remain non-diagnostic and tied to observable region paths.
evidence.changedCase: A second branch navigation and service outage region are added.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Landmark count predicts task success.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one task through regions and headings.

interactions.title: Repair changed catalog images
interactions.learnerAction: Code contextual alternatives or empty decoration for covers, format icons, availability marks, maps, and linked images, then connect remaining task barriers to bounded evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Catalog image access path is repaired.

evidence.observable: Image purpose and task equivalent remain correct after resource failure and purpose change.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative format icon becomes the only audiobook filter control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Object description is always the best alternative.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify task purpose before wording.

interactions.title: Test booking recovery tasks
interactions.learnerAction: Test multiple changed booking errors through keyboard, zoom, interruption, speech-input naming, and retained-state tasks; distinguish technical evidence from user assumptions.
interactions.artifactChange: Booking recovery task evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Users can perceive, locate, understand, correct, and confirm without cleared valid data or duplicate booking.
evidence.changedCase: Room capacity and date errors arrive after an interrupted retry.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Error announcement proves recovery for all contexts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace the complete correction journey.

interactions.title: Complete catalog table task evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Complete header-context, zoom, copy, comparison, and table-navigation evidence for changed title, format, branch, availability, and due-date groups.
interactions.artifactChange: Catalog table task evidence is completed.

evidence.observable: Every comparison value retains exact header context across navigation and narrow-width use.
evidence.changedCase: Formats split into digital and physical subgroup columns.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible column alignment serves every comparison strategy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test exact cell contexts and tasks.

interactions.title: Repair event-media equivalents
interactions.learnerAction: Code task-appropriate captions, transcript access, visual-information treatment, controls, and language for a changed library event recording, then state evidence limits.
interactions.artifactChange: Library event media path is repaired.

evidence.observable: Speech, sounds, visuals, timing, controls, and task information remain available through required equivalents.
evidence.changedCase: Slides gain essential silent demonstrations while questions become multilingual.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One transcript link completes all media access.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate information channels, timing, and tasks.

interactions.title: Explain library task uncertainty
interactions.learnerAction: Explain observed barriers, possible strategies, standards relevance, design response, evidence source, assumptions, sample limits, and unresolved risks for changed library users and contexts.
interactions.artifactChange: Bounded library task defense is committed.

evidence.observable: No disability category, tool, simulation, or single success becomes a universal conclusion.
evidence.changedCase: A novice touch user and expert switch user report different barriers.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Persona coverage equals user evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound each claim to task and observation.

interactions.title: Complete catalog exposure trace
interactions.learnerAction: Complete source, DOM, role, name, description, state, property, value, relationship, platform exposure, and observed-output traces for changed catalog controls and results.

evidence.observable: Every layer and environment-dependent observation is recorded separately with first divergence.
evidence.changedCase: A native select becomes custom cards and parser repair moves a label.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Screenshot and tag list predict semantic output.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one layer at a time.

interactions.title: Restore library native semantics
interactions.learnerAction: Replace style-only result headings, links, filters, disclosure, status, group, and table structures with truthful native HTML while preserving actual behavior and content.

evidence.observable: Browser exposes correct roles, relationships, names, states, values, structure, and native operation without redundant ARIA.
evidence.changedCase: A second results section and disabled filter state are introduced.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ARIA role repair is always equivalent.
interactions.correctionRoute: Select native element from behavior and relationship.

interactions.title: Test library semantic transitions
interactions.learnerAction: Test filter, disclosure, booking, error, status, media, and result changes while comparing visual, DOM, accessibility exposure, focus, request, and task state.
interactions.artifactChange: Library semantic transition evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Every exposed state and relationship stays synchronized through success, failure, reset, and reload.
evidence.changedCase: A filter request fails, retries, resets, and restores saved state.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Initial tree snapshot proves dynamic correctness.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace every transition and result.

interactions.title: Complete library name computations
interactions.learnerAction: Complete computed names and descriptions for search, repeated reserve links, format icons, groups, tables, figures, regions, help, and errors under changed native and ARIA sources.

evidence.observable: Winning source, precedence, role allowance, visible alignment, language, distinction, and description boundary are exact.
evidence.changedCase: Repeated Reserve labels translate and one stale hidden label remains.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All referenced text becomes a helpful name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply role and source precedence.

interactions.title: Repair library identity and help
interactions.learnerAction: Code visible native names for controls and regions, separate format instructions, branch context, current errors, and availability help as descriptions, and remove harmful descendant overrides.
interactions.artifactChange: Library names and descriptions are repaired.

evidence.observable: Every object is concise, distinguishable, visibly aligned, translatable, and supplemented without name inflation.
evidence.changedCase: Help doubles, one error clears, and two branches gain identical actions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Long invisible labels maximize information.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate identity, context, and changing guidance.

interactions.title: Test changed library names
interactions.learnerAction: Test computed names, descriptions, controls-only and links-only output, speech-input visible wording, translation, content changes, hidden references, and resource failure.
interactions.artifactChange: Library naming regression evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Identity and supplementary context remain exact under every changed source and environment.
evidence.changedCase: Titles, branch names, language, icons, errors, and hidden help all change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One screen-reader announcement proves naming.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect computed sources and task distinction.

interactions.title: Complete hidden-state matrix
interactions.learnerAction: Complete rendering, layout, DOM, tree, focus, form, name-reference, find, copy, print, and task-meaning outcomes for changed library hidden, inert, CSS-hidden, aria-hidden, and visually hidden content.

evidence.observable: Each mechanism has exact independent outcomes and no excluded focus or unique meaning.
evidence.changedCase: A hidden booking step gains an error target and status update.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One hidden concept applies to every mechanism.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate every dimension separately.

interactions.title: Repair library conditional content
interactions.learnerAction: Code closed panels, unavailable booking steps, redundant icons, visible skip links, and status content so rendering, tree, focus, form, naming, and task states remain synchronized.

evidence.observable: Every available task has one complete representation and excluded content has no focusable or uniquely meaningful descendant.
evidence.changedCase: A panel closes with focus inside and later becomes available again.

interactions.feedbackTarget: aria-hidden alone manages interactive availability.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use native state and test every dimension.

interactions.title: Test library inclusion transitions
interactions.learnerAction: Test forward, reverse, direct, restored, and error focus plus rendering, tree, form, naming, and persistence while content opens, closes, disables, removes, reorders, and reloads.

evidence.observable: Focus and unique meaning remain only in available content through every state transition.
evidence.changedCase: The active reservation step is removed during a saved-state reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tab traversal covers all inclusion failures.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test every focus entry and state transition.

interactions.title: Complete library order traces
interactions.learnerAction: Complete source, meaningful reading, visual, accessibility, and focus orders for changed search, filters, results, booking, help, errors, status, and responsive layouts.

evidence.observable: Dependencies and operations stay coherent with native focus, visible unobscured indicators, and no numeric repair.
evidence.changedCase: Filters move visually after results and a status action is inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All five orders must always match exactly.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve task dependencies in each presentation.

interactions.title: Repair library source and focus
interactions.learnerAction: Reorder source, restore native controls, remove positive tabindex, bound focus movement, preserve return targets, and keep focus visible through reflow, sticky content, errors, and disclosures.
interactions.artifactChange: Library order and focus are repaired.

evidence.observable: Keyboard tasks follow logical operation without trap, lost context, hidden destination, or obstruction.
evidence.changedCase: A new error summary and mobile sticky action enter the flow.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tabindex values and focus calls can repair any order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair source and native semantics before focus management.

interactions.title: Test complete library keyboard path
interactions.learnerAction: Complete search, filter, inspect, reserve, correct, confirm, cancel, reverse, escape, and resume tasks by keyboard at narrow width and high zoom while recording focus and results.
interactions.artifactChange: Library keyboard and focus evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Meaning, operation, focus visibility, context, and continuation survive changed content and layout.
evidence.changedCase: Results double, controls wrap, and a sticky notice obscures the lower viewport.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Successful tab sequence proves complete keyboard task.
interactions.correctionRoute: Exercise every action, state, escape, and continuation.

interactions.title: Repair library ARIA boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Replace partial custom widgets with native controls, retain only necessary supported relationships, synchronize changing states, and remove redundant, conflicting, prohibited, or stale ARIA.

evidence.observable: Every retained semantic promise has complete behavior and every native alternative supplies truthful default operation.
evidence.changedCase: A filter disables, a panel changes owner, and scripting fails during reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: More explicit roles and properties are safer.
interactions.correctionRoute: Prefer native; prove every remaining promise.

interactions.title: Test library behavior contract
interactions.learnerAction: Test role, name, state, property, value, focus, keyboard, pointer, touch, disabled, reset, error, result, escape, return, zoom, translation, and script-loss behavior for changed library interactions.
interactions.artifactChange: Library ARIA behavior evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Semantic exposure and interaction remain synchronized under every changed state or the pattern is rejected.
evidence.changedCase: Items reorder, labels translate, one item disables, and a handler disappears.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Role plus keyboard happy path completes behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test complete role promise and all transitions.

interactions.title: Defend library evaluation evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Run and explain complementary standards, automation, semantic, name, inclusion, keyboard, zoom, responsive, media, form, table, assistive-technology, and bounded observed-task evidence with limits and retests.
interactions.artifactChange: Bounded library accessibility report is committed.

evidence.observable: Claims match reproducible scope and evidence, avoid scores and universals, preserve failures and unknowns, and name next checks.
evidence.changedCase: Automation stays clean while a changed zoom and novice booking task fail.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Passing tools and one participant prove accessibility.
interactions.correctionRoute: Scope each conclusion to capable evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A defect clinic records causal diagnoses and repairs for interacting semantic, naming, hiding, focus, ARIA, and evaluation conflicts.
activityDesigns.startingState: Fourteen unrelated reduced cases each contain a plausible surface fix and a deeper evidence conflict.
activityDesigns.completionState: Every repair changes the cause, preserves prior correct behavior, and passes a new mutation.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback exposes only the first divergent observable after the learner commits a diagnosis.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints identify the next evidence layer, never the target markup.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Symptom-only patches are rejected and routed to causal trace reconstruction.

interactions.title: Predict stereotype-driven repair harm
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how one-tool disability assumptions misrepair regions, images, error recovery, result tables, and media while hiding the actual changed task barrier.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies specific task, observed barrier, harmful assumption, affected prior competency, and missing evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A screen-reader-only patch reaches keyboard, zoom, speech, and caption users.

interactions.feedbackTarget: More disability-specific branches improve coverage.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to shared task evidence and possible strategies.

interactions.title: Debug one-person universal claim
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a report that generalizes one expert participant's success to all disabled people, all tasks, conformance, and every assistive technology while retaining the useful observation.

evidence.observable: Report preserves exact participant, experience, environment, task, result, barrier, repair, and uncertainty without universal inference.
evidence.changedCase: A novice participant using another input method encounters a new barrier.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Discard limited evidence because it cannot generalize.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep observation; narrow claim; add complementary evidence.

interactions.title: Locate semantic-layer divergence
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a styled generic control through source, repaired DOM, computed role, name, state, platform exposure, focus, activation, form behavior, and observed output to locate the first failing layer.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis separates author semantics, browser mapping, environment presentation, and behavior consequences.
evidence.changedCase: A role is added but native disabled and form behavior remain absent.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Screen-reader wording identifies the whole cause.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace from source through every consumer layer.

interactions.title: Debug parser and exposure mismatch
interactions.learnerAction: Repair invalid nesting and duplicate IDs that move labels, controls, headings, and table headers in the DOM, then verify semantic exposure instead of patching the accessibility tree with ARIA.

evidence.observable: Conforming DOM yields intended roles, relationships, names, states, focus, and table context after mutation.
evidence.changedCase: A new nested control and duplicated header identifier are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ARIA references can override any malformed DOM.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair source and parsed relationships first.

interactions.title: Predict name override loss
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which descendant text, image purpose, error guidance, table caption, and visible label disappear or move when aria-label and aria-labelledby override changed objects.

evidence.observable: Exact winning name, separate description, suppressed descendant content, and task consequence are identified.
evidence.changedCase: A stale invisible label overrides translated visible content and current error text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ARIA naming only adds extra information.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compute name and description under role-specific precedence.

interactions.title: Debug duplicated and bloated names
interactions.learnerAction: Repair repeated role words, surrounding paragraphs, hidden duplicate labels, filenames, placeholder-only controls, stale translations, and error text embedded in names while preserving task distinction.
interactions.artifactChange: Name and description conflicts are repaired.

evidence.observable: Visible native-first names are concise and distinct; supplementary and changing information remains separately associated.
evidence.changedCase: Two identical actions, a translated label, and a cleared error are introduced.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Longer computed strings are always more accessible.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore purpose identity, distinction, visible alignment, and description boundary.

interactions.title: Locate hidden-focus conflict
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a visually absent but tabbable action and an aria-hidden subtree containing unique status, caption, transcript, and error content across rendering, tree, focus, form, naming, and meaning dimensions.

evidence.observable: Each unavailable focus stop and lost unique information is tied to exact exclusion mechanism and state transition.
evidence.changedCase: A collapsed media panel gains a retry button and new transcript section.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One aria-hidden removal fixes every hidden-state issue.
interactions.correctionRoute: Diagnose independent dimensions and task availability.

interactions.title: Debug conditional-step exclusion
interactions.learnerAction: Repair hidden, inert, disabled, removed, offscreen, and aria-hidden states in a conditional form step so focus, values, errors, labels, status, and task meaning synchronize through entry and exit.

evidence.observable: Unavailable content leaves no focus or stale request consequence; available content restores complete context and operation.
evidence.changedCase: The active step becomes unavailable after validation and returns after correction.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS display state alone manages full form availability.
interactions.correctionRoute: Model each state dimension and transition.

interactions.title: Predict visual-order trap
interactions.learnerAction: Predict reading, focus, and task consequences when CSS order, positive tabindex, fixed actions, hidden controls, and scripted focus make the visual sequence appear correct at one viewport.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies meaningful dependencies, first illogical transition, obstruction, lost context, and breakpoint mutation.
evidence.changedCase: A desktop two-column flow stacks and inserts a new conditional control.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Matching desktop tab and visual order proves durability.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace source and every order under mutation.

interactions.title: Debug keyboard continuation
interactions.learnerAction: Repair nested focusables, positive tabindex, focus theft, removed active targets, obscured indicators, traps, hover-only actions, missing escape, and wrong return focus across a changed task.

evidence.observable: Forward, reverse, activation, escape, removal, error, return, and narrow-width paths preserve meaning, operation, visibility, and context.
evidence.changedCase: An overlay opens during validation while sticky content obscures the return target.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding more focus calls controls the experience.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore native order and justify only task-required focus movement.

interactions.title: Inspect false ARIA promise
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a custom widget whose role, name, required states, supported properties, keyboard model, focus pattern, disabled behavior, pointer result, escape, and script-loss behavior disagree.
